title: plazia spartioides
intro: Plazia spartioides, also known as Desert Marigold, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe wounds and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Plazia spartioides include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made into teas, infusions, or salves, and can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
